meta.description: Complete curated Sysmon Sigma rule database for DFIR reference. Covers all Sysmon event types with behavioral detection patterns. Not a mirror of the full SigmaHQ repo — rules are hand-curated for clarity and investigative value.

rules.title: LOLBin — Certutil Download or Decode
rules.description: Detects abuse of certutil.exe for file download (-urlcache -split -f), base64 decode, or hash verification — all commonly used for payload delivery.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with certutil.exe; CommandLine contains -urlcache or -decode or -decodehex or -verifyctl, or URL patterns (http/ftp)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate IT use of certutil for certificate management. Tune by excluding known-good command patterns.

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
rules.description: Detects PowerShell execution with encoded commands (-EncodedCommand, -enc, -e) or obfuscated invocation flags, used to hide malicious scripts from command-line logging.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with powershell.exe or pwsh.exe; CommandLine contains -EncodedCommand or -enc or -e followed by base64 string, or hidden window flags (-WindowStyle Hidden, -NonI, -NoP)
rules.false_positives: Some legitimate applications and scripts use encoded commands. Tune by allowlisting known-good base64 patterns.

rules.title: Office Application Spawning Suspicious Child Process
rules.description: Detects Office applications (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint) spawning scripting interpreters, command shells, or other suspicious child processes — a primary macro/exploit delivery indicator.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage ends with WINWORD.EXE/EXCEL.EXE/POWERPNT.EXE/OUTLOOK.EXE; NewProcessName in [cmd.exe, powershell.exe, wscript.exe, cscript.exe, mshta.exe, certutil.exe, regsvr32.exe, wmic.exe, bitsadmin.exe, curl.exe, wget.exe]
rules.false_positives: Some legitimate macros spawn cmd/PowerShell for legitimate automation. Context-dependent.

rules.title: Mshta Executing Script Content
rules.description: Detects mshta.exe executing HTA content from remote URLs, inline VBScript/JScript, or suspicious file paths — a common phishing payload delivery technique.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with mshta.exe; CommandLine contains http:// or https:// or javascript: or vbscript: or .hta from temp/appdata paths
rules.false_positives: Legitimate HTA applications — rare in modern environments.

rules.title: Regsvr32 Executing Remote Scriptlet (Squiblydoo)
rules.description: Detects the Squiblydoo technique: regsvr32 loading a COM scriptlet from a remote URL or an unusual local path, executing arbitrary code while bypassing AppLocker.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with regsvr32.exe; CommandLine contains /s /u /i: or /s /i: followed by http:// or \\UNC path or .sct extension
rules.false_positives: Legitimate COM registration — should not include remote URLs.

rules.title: WMI Spawning Command Shell — Lateral Movement
rules.description: Detects WMI-based remote execution (wmiexec, Invoke-WmiMethod) producing cmd.exe or powershell.exe as a child of WmiPrvSE.exe.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage ends with WmiPrvSE.exe; NewProcessName in [cmd.exe, powershell.exe, cscript.exe, wscript.exe] with unexpected CommandLine
rules.false_positives: Legitimate WMI-based management (SCCM, monitoring agents). Tune by excluding known management system source IPs.

rules.title: Renamed Legitimate Binary Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of legitimate Windows binaries that have been renamed — a common technique to evade detection rules that match on process name. OriginalFileName differs from Image name.
rules.key_conditions: OriginalFileName in [cmd.exe, powershell.exe, cscript.exe, wscript.exe, mshta.exe, regsvr32.exe, rundll32.exe, schtasks.exe, net.exe] but Image name is different
rules.false_positives: Very rare legitimate renaming. High confidence detection.

rules.title: LSASS Dump via Command Line Tools
rules.description: Detects credential dumping tools invoked via command line: procdump targeting lsass, task manager dump, comsvcs.dll MiniDump, or direct LSASS access utilities.
rules.key_conditions: CommandLine contains procdump and lsass, or comsvcs.dll and MiniDump and lsass, or ProcDump and -ma, or werfault -s -p [lsass PID]
rules.false_positives: Legitimate debugging of LSASS by security tools. Very rare.

rules.title: Schtasks Creating Persistent Scheduled Task
rules.description: Detects scheduled task creation via schtasks.exe with suspicious parameters: tasks running from temp/appdata, encoded commands, or at system boot.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with schtasks.exe; CommandLine contains /create and (/sc onstart or /sc onlogon) and (cmd /c or powershell or %TEMP% or %APPDATA%)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate scheduled task creation by installers and management tools.

rules.title: Net.exe Adding User to Administrators Group
rules.description: Detects net.exe or net1.exe adding a user account to the Administrators local group — a backdoor account creation technique used by ransomware operators.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with net.exe or net1.exe; CommandLine contains localgroup and Administrators and /add
rules.false_positives: Legitimate admin operations. Alert and review all occurrences.

rules.title: PsExec Execution — Local or Remote
rules.description: Detects PsExec execution via OriginalFileName (psexec.exe) regardless of renaming, or the PSEXESVC.exe service being started on the target machine.
rules.key_conditions: OriginalFileName=psexec.exe or Image ends with PSEXESVC.exe, or ParentImage is services.exe with PSEXESVC in CommandLine
rules.false_positives: Legitimate administrative PsExec use. Alert and review.

rules.title: Whoami Execution — Reconnaissance
rules.description: Detects execution of whoami.exe, which attackers run immediately after gaining access to confirm their identity and privileges on the compromised host.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with whoami.exe; especially when ParentImage is cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or a suspicious process
rules.false_positives: Legitimate admin use. Tune by correlating with other recon activity.

rules.title: BloodHound/SharpHound AD Reconnaissance
rules.description: Detects SharpHound (BloodHound collector) execution via OriginalFileName, command-line flags, or characteristic output filenames.
rules.key_conditions: Image contains SharpHound or OriginalFileName=SharpHound; or CommandLine contains --CollectionMethods or -c All or --outputdirectory and --zipfilename; or file creation of *_computers.json or *_BloodHound.zip
rules.false_positives: Authorized red team operations.

rules.title: PowerShell Making Outbound Network Connection
rules.description: Detects PowerShell or pwsh making outbound network connections, indicating download cradle, C2 callback, or lateral movement activity.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with powershell.exe or pwsh.exe; Initiated=true; DestinationPort in [80,443,8080,8443] or any non-standard port; DestinationIp not in internal ranges
rules.false_positives: PowerShell-based management tools, Windows Update via PS, legitimate PS remoting.

rules.title: Rundll32 Outbound Network Connection
rules.description: Detects rundll32.exe initiating outbound network connections — highly suspicious as rundll32 has no legitimate reason to make external connections except in very specific scenarios.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with rundll32.exe; Initiated=true; DestinationPort in [80,443,4444,8080] or any external IP
rules.false_positives: Some Windows system operations (Windows Update, WPAD). Tune by excluding known-good destination IPs.

rules.title: Ngrok Tunnel Network Connection
rules.description: Detects network connections to Ngrok infrastructure (*.ngrok.io, *.ngrok-free.app, tunnel.us.ngrok.com) used to expose internal services or create reverse tunnels for C2.
rules.key_conditions: DestinationHostname ends with ngrok.io or ngrok-free.app or contains ngrok; DestinationPort in [443, 4443]
rules.false_positives: Legitimate developer use of Ngrok for testing. Rare in enterprise production environments.

rules.title: Suspicious Outbound SMTP Connection
rules.description: Detects non-mail-client processes making outbound SMTP connections (port 25/465/587) — indicates data exfiltration via email or spam sending.
rules.key_conditions: DestinationPort in [25,465,587]; Image not in known mail clients [outlook.exe, thunderbird.exe]; Initiated=true
rules.false_positives: On-premises mail servers, legitimate mail relay services.

rules.title: Mimikatz SSP DLL Load
rules.description: Detects loading of Mimikatz SSP (Security Support Provider) DLLs into LSASS — mimilib.dll or custom SSP DLLs that log all LSASS authentication events to disk.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with lsass.exe; ImageLoaded contains mimilib.dll or matches unsigned DLL loaded into LSASS from non-system32 path; SignatureStatus not Signed
rules.false_positives: Very rare. Legitimate SSP DLLs are always signed Microsoft binaries.

rules.title: Credential Dumping DLL Loaded by Non-System Process
rules.description: Detects credential-related DLLs (wce.dll, wce32.dll, or known dumper DLLs) loaded by unexpected processes.
rules.key_conditions: ImageLoaded contains wce.dll, wce32.dll, or matches hash of known credential dumping DLLs; loaded by non-system processes
rules.false_positives: Extremely rare legitimate use.

rules.title: Injection into LSASS via CreateRemoteThread
rules.description: Detects process injection into lsass.exe via CreateRemoteThread — a primary technique for in-memory credential theft without touching disk.
rules.key_conditions: TargetImage ends with lsass.exe; StartModule is null or empty (no backing module — shellcode injection); SourceImage is not a known security tool
rules.false_positives: Some AV/EDR vendors inject into LSASS for monitoring. Build allowlist of known security products.

rules.title: CreateRemoteThread into Suspicious Target Process
rules.description: Detects injection into browser processes, explorer.exe, or other commonly hollowed targets using CreateRemoteThread. StartModule=null indicates shellcode.
rules.key_conditions: TargetImage ends with [explorer.exe, svchost.exe, dllhost.exe, chrome.exe, firefox.exe]; StartModule empty or null; SourceImage not in allowlist
rules.false_positives: AV/EDR injection for monitoring. Exclude known security product processes.

rules.title: LSASS Memory Access with Credential Dump Mask
rules.description: Detects LSASS process access with GrantedAccess masks characteristic of credential dumpers (Mimikatz, Cobalt Strike, CrackMapExec). High-confidence detection.
rules.key_conditions: TargetImage ends with lsass.exe; GrantedAccess in [0x1010, 0x1410, 0x1438, 0x143a, 0x1fffff, 0x1f1fff]; SourceImage not in [known AV/EDR processes]
rules.false_positives: AV/EDR monitoring of LSASS. Build tight allowlist for your security stack.

rules.title: Credential Manager Access via Process Open
rules.description: Detects unexpected process access to Credential Manager (vaultcli.dll loaded or vault-related process access), used to steal stored Windows credentials.
rules.key_conditions: TargetImage ends with explorer.exe or consent.exe; GrantedAccess has PROCESS_VM_READ; CallTrace contains vaultcli.dll or dpapi.dll
rules.false_positives: Legitimate credential backup/restore operations.

rules.title: Executable Created in Suspicious Location
rules.description: Detects PE executables (.exe, .dll, .sys) created in high-risk directories: %TEMP%, %APPDATA%, %PUBLIC%, \ProgramData, \Users\Public — common malware drop locations.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .exe or .dll or .sys; TargetFilename starts with C:\Windows\Temp or C:\Users\*\AppData\Local\Temp or C:\Users\Public or C:\ProgramData
rules.false_positives: Some legitimate installers use temp directories. Tune by excluding known software installers.

rules.title: Startup Folder File Created — Persistence
rules.technique: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
rules.description: Detects file creation in Windows Startup folders — a simple persistence mechanism that executes files at user logon.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename contains \Start Menu\Programs\Startup\ or \Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\StartUp\; created by non-installer process
rules.false_positives: Legitimate software using startup folder (OneDrive, Teams, etc.). Tune by excluding known-good application paths.

rules.title: Run Key Persistence — Registry Modification
rules.description: Detects creation or modification of Run/RunOnce registry keys — the most common persistence mechanism.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains \CurrentVersion\Run or \CurrentVersion\RunOnce; EventType in [CreateValue, SetValue]; Image not in [msiexec.exe, known installers]
rules.false_positives: Software installation commonly writes Run keys. Build an allowlist of known-good values.

rules.title: WDigest Credential Caching Enabled via Registry
rules.description: Detects enabling of WDigest credential caching (UseLogonCredential=1) which forces cleartext password storage in LSASS memory for Mimikatz harvesting.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains WDigest\UseLogonCredential; Details=1 (DWORD value set to 1); EventType=SetValue
rules.false_positives: Extremely rare legitimate use. Alert on all occurrences.

rules.title: IFEO Debugger Persistence or Accessibility Backdoor
rules.description: Detects Image File Execution Options Debugger value being set — used for accessibility backdoors (sethc.exe→cmd.exe) and general process redirect persistence.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains Image File Execution Options and Debugger; EventType in [CreateValue, SetValue]; value not pointing to legitimate debugger
rules.false_positives: Legitimate debugging configurations by developers.

rules.title: Windows Defender Real-Time Protection Disabled
rules.technique: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
rules.description: Detects disabling of Windows Defender real-time protection via registry — a prerequisite step by ransomware and malware before payload execution.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains Windows Defender\Real-Time Protection\DisableRealtimeMonitoring; Details=1; or DisableAntiSpyware=1; EventType=SetValue
rules.false_positives: Security team testing, managed AV replacement workflows.

rules.title: Suspicious Download via Browser — MOTW Zone.Identifier
rules.description: Detects executable files downloaded via browser with Zone.Identifier ADS, capturing the source URL for phishing investigation. Correlates file hash with threat intel.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .exe or .zip or .iso or .lnk; Stream contains Zone.Identifier; Contents includes HostUrl or ReferrerUrl from suspicious domains
rules.false_positives: All browser downloads trigger this — tune by focusing on executable types and suspicious URL patterns.

rules.title: Cobalt Strike Default Named Pipe Patterns
rules.description: Detects Cobalt Strike C2 beacon characteristic named pipe patterns used for inter-process communication between beacon stages.
rules.key_conditions: PipeName matches patterns: \\pipe\\msagent_*, \\pipe\\status_*, \\pipe\\MSSE-*, \\pipe\\postex_*, \\pipe\\postex_ssh_*, \\pipe\\dce_*, \\pipe\\mojo_*
rules.false_positives: Highly characteristic — very low false positive rate for Cobalt Strike default configurations.

rules.title: PsExec Network Pipe Communication
rules.description: Detects PsExec communication pipes created by the PSEXESVC service on the target machine during remote execution.
rules.key_conditions: PipeName matches \\PSEXESVC* or \\PAExec* or \\RemCom*; Image ends with PSEXESVC.exe or services.exe
rules.false_positives: Legitimate PsExec use by administrators.

rules.title: WMI Event Subscription — Fileless Persistence
rules.technique: Event Triggered Execution: WMI Event Subscription
rules.description: Detects WMI event subscription creation (filter + consumer + binding) used for fileless persistence that survives reboots without touching the filesystem.
rules.key_conditions: EID 19: Filter with suspicious trigger (SELECT * FROM Win32_LogonSession, __InstanceCreationEvent); EID 20: CommandLineEventConsumer or ActiveScriptEventConsumer with malicious payload in Destination; EID 21: binding activating the subscription
rules.false_positives: Legitimate WMI subscriptions by management software (SCCM). Build an allowlist.

rules.title: LOLBin Making Suspicious DNS Query
rules.description: Detects living-off-the-land binaries (certutil, regsvr32, mshta, rundll32, powershell) making DNS queries to external domains — C2 communication or download cradle.
rules.key_conditions: Image in [certutil.exe, regsvr32.exe, mshta.exe, rundll32.exe, powershell.exe]; QueryName not in internal DNS zones; QueryStatus=0 (success)
rules.false_positives: Windows Update checking may use PowerShell DNS. Tune by allowlisting known Windows Update domains.

rules.title: DGA Domain Query — High-Entropy Domain Name
rules.description: Detects DNS queries to high-entropy domain names characteristic of Domain Generation Algorithm (DGA) C2 — random-looking subdomains that are hard to blocklist.
rules.key_conditions: QueryName has high character entropy (Shannon entropy > 3.5) or matches DGA patterns (long random subdomain, many numeric chars); multiple rapid queries to different high-entropy domains
rules.false_positives: Some CDN domains have high entropy. Tune with legitimate CDN/cloud domain allowlist.

rules.title: Vulnerable Driver Loaded (LOLDrivers / BYOVD)
rules.description: Detects loading of known vulnerable or malicious drivers used in Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) attacks to disable security tools or gain ring-0 access.
rules.key_conditions: ImageLoaded hash matches known vulnerable driver hashes from loldrivers.io; or Signed=false; or SignatureStatus=revoked; or driver loaded from suspicious path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate but outdated signed drivers — validate against loldrivers.io blocklist.

rules.title: Unsigned or Revoked Driver Loaded
rules.description: Detects loading of unsigned or revoked-signature kernel drivers — rootkits and malicious drivers are either unsigned or use stolen/revoked certificates.
rules.key_conditions: Signed=false or SignatureStatus=revoked or SignatureStatus=errorCode_endpoint; driver not in known-good driver allowlist
rules.false_positives: Older legitimate drivers may lack signatures. Build allowlist of known-good unsigned drivers.

rules.title: Process Hollowing or Herpaderping Detected
rules.description: Detects process image manipulation: hollowing (replacing legitimate process memory with malicious code) or herpaderping (modifying file after process creation). Sysmon natively detects both.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=25; Type in ['Image is replaced','Image is locked for access']; any process — near-zero legitimate rate
rules.false_positives: Extremely rare. Some older software protection schemes may trigger herpaderping detection.

rules.title: Raw Volume Read — Credential File Access
rules.description: Detects raw disk volume reads by non-backup processes — used to access locked credential files (NTDS.dit, SAM hive, SECURITY hive) without triggering file system auditing.
rules.key_conditions: Device contains PhysicalDrive or HarddiskVolume; Image not in [known backup tools]; Image not System
rules.false_positives: Backup software, disk analysis tools. Build tight allowlist.

rules.title: Sysmon Service Stopped or Config Modified
rules.technique: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
rules.description: Detects Sysmon service being stopped (EID 4, State=Stopped) or configuration modified unexpectedly (EID 16). Attackers stop or reconfigure Sysmon to blind monitoring.
rules.key_conditions: EID 4 with State=Stopped (outside of scheduled maintenance windows); or EID 16 during unexpected time periods or by unexpected accounts
rules.false_positives: Planned Sysmon maintenance, configuration updates by security team. Suppress during known change windows.

rules.title: Cloudflared Tunnel — C2 or Data Exfiltration via Cloudflare
rules.description: Detects execution of Cloudflared, the Cloudflare tunnel client, which attackers use to expose internal services or create persistent reverse tunnels. Observed in multiple ransomware intrusions (LockBit, Play) as an alternative to Ngrok.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with cloudflared.exe or OriginalFileName=cloudflared.exe; CommandLine contains tunnel or --url or access
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Cloudflare Tunnel deployments in organizations using Cloudflare Zero Trust.

rules.title: Impacket Lateral Movement Tool Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of compiled Impacket tools (wmiexec, smbexec, atexec, dcomexec, secretsdump) by filename pattern or characteristic command-line arguments. The /Q /c echo pattern is signature Impacket wmiexec output redirection.
rules.key_conditions: Image name contains impacket or wmiexec or smbexec or atexec or dcomexec; or CommandLine contains /Q /c echo and \\127.0.0.1\ or C$\\__output; or ParentImage=wmiprvse.exe with cmd.exe child and echo redirect
rules.false_positives: Authorized red team operations. The /Q /c echo pattern is highly characteristic.

rules.title: AdFind Active Directory Reconnaissance
rules.description: Detects AdFind execution — a single-binary AD query tool that is a signature tool in Conti, LockBit, and Ryuk pre-ransomware reconnaissance. Detected by filename, OriginalFileName, or characteristic command-line flags.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with adfind.exe or AdFind.exe; or CommandLine contains -f objectcategory or -sc dclist or -subt or -csv; or OriginalFileName=adfind.exe
rules.false_positives: Authorized AD administrators using AdFind for legitimate queries.

rules.title: SDelete Secure File Deletion — Anti-Forensics
rules.description: Detects SDelete (Sysinternals) execution used by attackers to securely delete files and prevent recovery. Ransomware groups use SDelete post-encryption to destroy backups and forensic evidence.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with sdelete.exe or sdelete64.exe; or OriginalFileName=sdelete.exe; CommandLine contains -p (overwrite passes) or -s (recursive) or *.* targeting directories
rules.false_positives: Legitimate secure file deletion by administrators (should be rare and documented).

rules.title: WMIC Remote Process Creation — Lateral Movement
rules.description: Detects WMIC used for remote process creation — a classic lateral movement technique. CommandLine includes node: (remote target), process call create, and a payload command.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with wmic.exe; CommandLine contains /node: and process and call and create; or CommandLine contains process call create with suspicious payload (powershell, cmd, cscript)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate WMIC-based remote management by IT operations.

rules.title: Network Reconnaissance via Built-in Tools
rules.description: Detects rapid sequential execution of native Windows network reconnaissance tools (net.exe, netstat, ipconfig, arp, nslookup, ping) — a pattern consistent with post-exploitation situational awareness.
rules.key_conditions: Image in [net.exe, net1.exe, netstat.exe, ipconfig.exe, arp.exe, nslookup.exe, ping.exe, tracert.exe, route.exe]; multiple distinct executions within short time window from same parent process
rules.false_positives: Legitimate admin network troubleshooting. Alert on burst patterns (5+ tools in <60 seconds).

rules.title: Shadow Copy Deletion — Ransomware Preparation
rules.description: Detects Volume Shadow Copy deletion via vssadmin, wmic, or PowerShell — the single most reliable pre-ransomware indicator. Nearly every ransomware family deletes VSS copies before encryption.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with vssadmin.exe with CommandLine contains delete shadows; or wmic.exe with shadowcopy and delete; or powershell.exe with Get-WmiObject Win32_ShadowCopy and .Delete(); or bcdedit with recoveryenabled no or ignoreallfailures
rules.false_positives: Legitimate backup rotation scripts. In most enterprise environments VSS deletion should require change control.

rules.title: Chisel Reverse Proxy Tunnel Execution
rules.description: Detects Chisel TCP/UDP tunnel execution. Chisel is a Go binary used by attackers for port forwarding and SOCKS proxy chains through firewalls. Often renamed but Go binary signatures are detectable.
rules.key_conditions: Image name contains chisel; or CommandLine contains client and R:socks or R:0.0.0.0 or server --port; or OriginalFileName=chisel (Go binary metadata)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate network tunneling for development environments. Rare in enterprise production.

rules.title: Procdump LSASS Memory Dump
rules.description: Detects ProcDump (Sysinternals) targeting LSASS process for memory dumping — a common credential theft technique that abuses a legitimate signed tool to evade AV.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with procdump.exe or procdump64.exe; CommandLine contains lsass; or CommandLine contains -ma and (lsass or process ID of lsass); or OriginalFileName=procdump
rules.false_positives: Legitimate debugging of LSASS by support engineers — extremely rare and should be via ticket.

rules.title: Suspicious Execution from Recycle Bin or Temp
rules.description: Detects process execution from highly suspicious locations: Recycle Bin ($Recycle.Bin), Windows Temp, system32 temp, or user temp directories. Malware frequently executes from these paths to evade detection.
rules.key_conditions: Image contains $Recycle.Bin or \Recycler\ or C:\Windows\Temp\ or \AppData\Local\Temp\ and file extension is .exe or .dll; CommandLine or Image path indicates temp-based execution
rules.false_positives: Some legitimate installers extract to and execute from temp. Tune by excluding known software updaters.

rules.title: Lateral Movement via Copy to Admin Share + Execution
rules.description: Detects the classic lateral movement pattern: copying an executable to a remote admin share followed by remote execution via sc.exe, psexec, or AT command — characteristic of ransomware operators and red teams.
rules.key_conditions: CommandLine contains copy and \\<target>\C$ or \\<target>\ADMIN$; or cmd.exe with copy and net use pattern; followed by sc.exe or schtasks creating remote task
rules.false_positives: Legitimate software deployment via admin shares (SCCM, PDQ Deploy).

rules.title: PowerShell Disable Defender via Set-MpPreference
rules.technique: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
rules.description: Detects PowerShell commands disabling Windows Defender features via Set-MpPreference — used by ransomware and malware to disable real-time protection before payload execution.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with powershell.exe or pwsh.exe; CommandLine contains Set-MpPreference and (DisableRealtimeMonitoring or DisableIOAVProtection or DisableBehaviorMonitoring or ExclusionPath or ExclusionProcess) with $true or 1
rules.false_positives: Security team testing, managed AV replacement procedures.

rules.title: Rclone Cloud Sync — Potential Data Exfiltration
rules.description: Detects Rclone execution used for exfiltrating data to cloud storage (MEGA, Dropbox, S3, Google Drive). Heavily used by ransomware operators (Scattered Spider, ALPHV, Cl0p) for large-volume exfiltration.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with rclone.exe or OriginalFileName=rclone.exe; CommandLine contains copy or sync or move with remote: pattern; or CommandLine contains --config and cloud provider flags (mega, s3, gdrive)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate IT use of Rclone for cloud backup. Alert on non-standard config files and unusual destination remotes.

rules.title: Suspicious High-Risk Port Outbound — C2 Beaconing
rules.description: Detects processes making outbound connections on non-standard high-risk ports commonly used by C2 frameworks: 4444 (Metasploit default), 4445, 1337, 8888, 9999, 6666, 50050 (Cobalt Strike teamserver).
rules.key_conditions: Initiated=true; DestinationPort in [4444,4445,1337,8888,9999,6666,6667,50050,55553]; Image not in known-good processes making these connections
rules.false_positives: Development environments using these ports. Tune by IP range — alert specifically on external IPs.

rules.title: Non-Browser Process Connecting to File Sharing Service
rules.description: Detects non-browser processes making connections to file sharing and cloud storage services (MEGA, WeTransfer, Dropbox, Google Drive, paste sites) that may indicate data exfiltration.
rules.key_conditions: Initiated=true; DestinationHostname ends with mega.nz or mega.io or wetransfer.com or paste.ee or pastebin.com or file.io or transfer.sh; Image not in [chrome.exe, firefox.exe, msedge.exe, brave.exe]
rules.false_positives: Enterprise sync clients. Tune by including expected sync tool processes in allowlist.

rules.title: Cobalt Strike Default Network Indicators
rules.description: Detects Cobalt Strike C2 network characteristics: connections on port 443/80 from unusual processes with beacon-like jitter intervals, or connections to known Cobalt Strike team server certificates.
rules.key_conditions: Image in [svchost.exe, rundll32.exe, dllhost.exe, regsvr32.exe, mshta.exe]; Initiated=true; DestinationPort in [80,443,8080,8443]; DestinationHostname not in known CDN/update domains
rules.false_positives: Windows system processes occasionally make legitimate HTTP connections. Build tight allowlist of expected destinations.

rules.title: DNS-over-HTTPS by Non-Browser — C2 Evasion
rules.description: Detects non-browser processes connecting to known DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) providers (Cloudflare 1.1.1.1, Google 8.8.8.8, Quad9) on port 443 — used by malware to bypass DNS monitoring and resolve C2 domains without hitting corporate DNS.
rules.key_conditions: Initiated=true; DestinationIp in [1.1.1.1, 1.0.0.1, 8.8.8.8, 8.8.4.4, 9.9.9.9, 149.112.112.112, 94.140.14.14]; DestinationPort=443; Image not in [chrome.exe, firefox.exe, msedge.exe, brave.exe]
rules.false_positives: Some applications with built-in DoH support. Tune by process allowlist.

rules.title: Suspicious DLL Loaded from AppData or Temp
rules.description: Detects DLL loading from user-writable high-risk paths (AppData, Temp, Downloads, Public) into processes — indicative of DLL hijacking or side-loading to execute malicious code.
rules.key_conditions: ImageLoaded starts with C:\Users\ and contains \AppData\ or \Temp\ or \Downloads\; or starts with C:\ProgramData\; file extension is .dll; Signed=false or from unexpected process
rules.false_positives: Some legitimate applications install DLLs in AppData. Build hash-based allowlist for known-good DLLs.

rules.title: DLL Loaded by Signed Binary with Unsigned DLL — Hijack
rules.technique: Hijack Execution Flow: DLL Search Order
rules.description: Detects when a trusted signed Windows binary loads an unsigned DLL from a non-standard path — the signature of DLL search-order hijacking where malicious DLLs are placed alongside legitimate executables.
rules.key_conditions: Image is a signed Microsoft/system binary; ImageLoaded path is NOT in C:\Windows\System32\ or C:\Windows\SysWOW64\; Signed=false or SignatureStatus != Valid
rules.false_positives: Some legitimate third-party software bundles unsigned DLLs with signed executables. Build path+process allowlist.

rules.title: PowerShell Reflection — In-Memory Assembly Load
rules.description: Detects PowerShell loading .NET assemblies reflectively (Invoke-ReflectivePEInjection, [System.Reflection.Assembly]::Load) — used to execute Mimikatz, Rubeus, and other tools entirely in memory without touching disk.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with powershell.exe or pwsh.exe; ImageLoaded path starts with C:\Windows\assembly\NativeImages or appears as a memory-backed module with no disk path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate PowerShell modules loaded in-process. Focus on unsigned in-memory assemblies.

rules.title: Suspicious Parent-Child Injection via CreateRemoteThread
rules.description: Detects injection from scripting engines (wscript, cscript, mshta, powershell) into system processes (svchost, taskhost, explorer) via CreateRemoteThread — characteristic of script-based malware pivoting to a persistent process.
rules.key_conditions: SourceImage ends with [wscript.exe, cscript.exe, mshta.exe, powershell.exe, cmd.exe]; TargetImage ends with [svchost.exe, taskhost.exe, explorer.exe, dllhost.exe]; StartModule empty
rules.false_positives: Rare. Some monitoring tools may inject via scripts into system processes.

rules.title: Token Impersonation via Process Open — Privilege Escalation
rules.description: Detects process access with PROCESS_DUP_HANDLE (0x40) or PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION access rights targeting high-privilege processes — used for token impersonation/theft to escalate privileges.
rules.key_conditions: GrantedAccess contains 0x40 (PROCESS_DUP_HANDLE) or 0x400 (PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION); TargetImage in [lsass.exe, winlogon.exe, services.exe]; SourceImage is unexpected non-system process
rules.false_positives: Security tools and debuggers legitimately query process information. Build allowlist.

rules.title: LNK File Created in Suspicious Location
rules.description: Detects LNK (shortcut) file creation in startup folders, desktop, or suspicious temp locations — used for persistence or as a delivery mechanism (ISO-based phishing drops LNK that executes payload).
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .lnk; location is Startup folder or Desktop or \Temp\ or \AppData\; created by unexpected process (not Windows Explorer, known apps)
rules.false_positives: Application installation creating legitimate shortcuts. Focus on temp-path LNK creation.

rules.title: Office Application Writing Executable to Disk
rules.description: Detects Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) writing PE executables (.exe, .dll) to disk — a clear indicator of macro-based malware dropper activity.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with WINWORD.EXE or EXCEL.EXE or POWERPNT.EXE or OUTLOOK.EXE; TargetFilename ends with .exe or .dll or .sys or .ps1 or .vbs or .js; TargetFilename NOT in known legitimate Office plugin paths
rules.false_positives: Office add-ins that install components — very rare and should be allowlisted.

rules.title: Script File Written to Windows Directory
rules.description: Detects script files (PowerShell, batch, VBScript, JScript) being written directly into C:\Windows\ or C:\Windows\System32\ — attackers place scripts here to abuse the trusted path and avoid detection.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename starts with C:\Windows\ and ends with .ps1 or .bat or .cmd or .vbs or .js or .hta; Image not in [msiexec.exe, TrustedInstaller.exe, System]
rules.false_positives: Windows updates occasionally place scripts here. Tune by excluding TrustedInstaller and SYSTEM write operations.

rules.title: Windows Defender Exclusion Path Added
rules.technique: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
rules.description: Detects addition of exclusion paths or processes to Windows Defender via registry — attackers add the directory containing their tools to Defender exclusions before dropping payloads.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains Windows Defender\Exclusions\Paths or Exclusions\Processes or Exclusions\Extensions; EventType in [CreateValue, SetValue]; Image not in [MsMpEng.exe, known AV management tools]
rules.false_positives: Legitimate AV exclusions configured by security team or via GPO. Alert on process context.

rules.title: UAC Disabled via Registry — EnableLUA=0
rules.description: Detects disabling of User Account Control (UAC) by setting EnableLUA=0 in registry — removes the primary elevation control mechanism, allowing silent privilege escalation.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains Policies\System\EnableLUA; Details=0 (DWORD); EventType=SetValue
rules.false_positives: Misconfigured enterprise deployments. Alert on all occurrences — EnableLUA should always be 1.

rules.title: Suspicious AutoRun Registry Key in Uncommon Path
rules.description: Detects autostart registry value pointing to executables in unusual locations (temp, appdata, programdata, recycle bin) rather than Program Files — a key persistence IOC.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains \CurrentVersion\Run; Details (value data) contains \Temp\ or \AppData\ or \ProgramData\ or $Recycle or \Users\Public; EventType=SetValue
rules.false_positives: Some legitimate applications install Run entries pointing to AppData (OneDrive, Dropbox). Tune by value name.

rules.title: Service Registry Key Modified — Potential Hijack
rules.technique: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
rules.description: Detects modification of a Windows service's ImagePath registry value — used to hijack existing legitimate services by redirecting to malicious binaries, or to plant new malicious services.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\ and ImagePath; EventType=SetValue; new ImagePath value points to unusual directory or contains powershell/cmd embedded commands; Image not msiexec.exe or sc.exe
rules.false_positives: Legitimate service installation and update. Alert on ImagePath values pointing outside Program Files or Windows.

rules.title: File Timestamp Modified — Timestomping
rules.description: Detects file creation time modification (timestomping) — attackers change file timestamps to match system files or predate the intrusion to confuse forensic timeline analysis.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=2; Image not in [known legitimate timestamping tools: robocopy.exe, xcopy.exe, backup software]; PreviousCreationUtcTime differs significantly from CreationUtcTime (e.g., changed to pre-2010 or future date)
rules.false_positives: File sync and backup tools legitimately modify timestamps. Focus on PE files and executables modified by unexpected processes.

rules.title: Malware Backdating Timestamp to System File Era
rules.description: Detects timestomping where an attacker sets a file's creation time to match a known Windows OS release date — a specific APT technique to make backdoors appear to be original system files.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=2; CreationUtcTime matches Windows OS release dates (e.g., 2009-07-14 for Win7, 2012-07-26 for Win8, 2015-07-29 for Win10) within a short date range; TargetFilename ends with .exe or .dll
rules.false_positives: Extremely rare legitimate use case.

rules.title: ISO or IMG File Download — MOTW Bypass Container
rules.description: Detects download of ISO or IMG disk image files which attackers use to deliver payloads while bypassing Mark of the Web propagation (files inside ISO do not inherit Zone.Identifier from the container).
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .iso or .img or .vhd or .vhdx; Stream=Zone.Identifier with ZoneId=3 (Internet); Image is browser or mail client
rules.false_positives: Legitimate ISO downloads for software installation. Alert on combined with suspicious URL patterns.

rules.title: Suspicious File Downloaded from Paste or File Sharing Site
rules.description: Detects executable or script files downloaded from paste/file sharing sites (pastebin, paste.ee, file.io, transfer.sh, raw.githubusercontent abused) — common payload delivery infrastructure.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .exe or .ps1 or .bat or .hta or .vbs or .js; Zone.Identifier Contents/HostUrl contains pastebin.com or paste.ee or file.io or transfer.sh or hastebin.com
rules.false_positives: Developers downloading legitimate scripts from GitHub gists or pastebin. Tune by executable file type focus.

rules.title: Sliver or Brute Ratel C2 DNS Beacon Patterns
rules.description: Detects DNS query patterns characteristic of Sliver C2 (random-looking subdomains, mTLS certificates) and Brute Ratel (characteristic DNS patterns). These frameworks generate DNS queries with high entropy subdomains.
rules.key_conditions: QueryName has high entropy subdomains (>3.5 Shannon entropy); or QueryName structure matches known Sliver patterns (random 8-12 char subdomain under attacker domain); rapid sequential queries to same base domain
rules.false_positives: Some CDNs generate high-entropy subdomains. Correlate with process and network context.

rules.title: Suspicious DNS Query to Newly Registered or Typosquatted Domain
rules.description: Detects DNS queries to domains matching known typosquatting patterns (microsoft-update.com, windowsupdate.net, etc.) or queries from processes that should never make external DNS queries.
rules.key_conditions: QueryName matches typosquatting patterns for common enterprise domains; or QueryName resolves to recently registered domain (TTI integration needed); Image in [wmic.exe, regsvr32.exe, rundll32.exe, mshta.exe]
rules.false_positives: Low — process-specific filtering keeps FP rate manageable.

rules.title: Ransomware Mass File Deletion Pattern
rules.description: Detects high-volume file deletion events characteristic of ransomware wiping original files post-encryption or a wiper malware destroying data. Alert on >100 file deletions per minute from a single process.
rules.key_conditions: EID 23 or 26; high volume of events from single ProcessGuid within short time window; TargetFilename extensions are common document types (.docx, .xlsx, .pdf, .jpg) being deleted in bulk
rules.false_positives: Legitimate bulk file cleanup operations. Alert on rate threshold combined with diverse file type coverage.

rules.title: Tool Cleanup — Attacker Deleting Own Executables
rules.description: Detects attackers deleting their own tools after use — IsExecutable=true in file deletion events from temp/appdata, combined with the deleting process being cmd.exe or PowerShell, is a strong post-exploitation cleanup IOC.
rules.key_conditions: EID 23 or 26; IsExecutable=true; TargetFilename path is \Temp\ or \AppData\ or \ProgramData\ or \Downloads\; Image in [cmd.exe, powershell.exe, wscript.exe]
rules.false_positives: Installers cleaning up temp executables after installation. Focus on temp-path PE deletions by scripting engines.

rules.title: PE File Created in Temp by Unexpected Process
rules.description: Detects new PE executable files created in temp/appdata directories by non-installer processes (EID 29 is PE-specific, lower noise than EID 11). Dropped executables in these paths indicate initial access payload staging.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=29; TargetFilename in \Temp\ or \AppData\ or \ProgramData\ or \Downloads\; Image not in [msiexec.exe, setup.exe, unzip.exe, 7z.exe, known updaters]
rules.false_positives: Software updaters and installers extract executables to temp. Build process-based allowlist.

rules.title: PE File Written by Browser or Mail Client
rules.description: Detects PE executable files written to disk by web browsers or mail clients (EID 29 — PE-specific detection) — a reliable indicator of file-based phishing delivery or drive-by download.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=29; Image ends with [chrome.exe, firefox.exe, msedge.exe, brave.exe, OUTLOOK.EXE, thunderbird.exe]; TargetFilename ends with .exe or .dll (PE files written by browser/mail)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate software downloaded through browser. Correlate with EID 15 Zone.Identifier for source URL.

rules.title: Raw Disk Read by Script or Office Process
rules.description: Detects raw physical disk reads from scripting engines or Office applications — much more suspicious than raw reads from dedicated tools. Indicates a script-embedded credential harvester.
rules.key_conditions: Device contains PhysicalDrive or HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy; Image ends with [powershell.exe, wscript.exe, cscript.exe, WINWORD.EXE, EXCEL.EXE, mshta.exe]
rules.false_positives: Essentially no legitimate use case for Office or PowerShell to perform raw disk reads.

rules.title: AbusedDebugPrivilege by Arbitrary Parent Processes
rules.description: Detects unusual child processes from system processes that normally don't spawn children. Indicates debug privilege abuse for arbitrary parent spoofing.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage in [fontdrvhost.exe,spoolsv.exe,csrss.exe] spawning unexpected child processes not normal for that parent
rules.false_positives: Rare legitimate use cases with custom system software

rules.title: AddinUtil.EXE Execution From Uncommon Directory
rules.description: Detects execution of AddInutil.exe (Add-In deployment cache updater) from non-standard directory — used as LOLBIN to execute arbitrary code.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with AddInutil.exe; CurrentDirectory or CommandLine path not in standard Windows directories
rules.false_positives: Legitimate .NET framework operations

rules.title: AgentExecutor PowerShell Execution
rules.description: Detects abuse of AgentExecutor.exe to run PowerShell with ExecutionPolicy Bypass or arbitrary binaries named powershell.exe.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with AgentExecutor.exe; CommandLine contains powershell or script path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Intune management agent operations

rules.title: Arbitrary File Download Via IMEWDBLD.EXE
rules.description: IMEWDBLD.exe (IME dictionary downloader) used as LOLBIN to download arbitrary files from remote URLs.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with IMEWDBLD.exe; CommandLine contains http:// or https://
rules.false_positives: Legitimate IME dictionary updates

rules.title: Arbitrary File Download Via MSPUB.EXE
rules.description: Microsoft Publisher (mspub.exe) used to download arbitrary files from remote URLs via embedded object.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with mspub.exe; ChildProcess or CommandLine involves remote URL; spawning unusual processes
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Publisher operations

rules.title: Always Install Elevated MSI Spawned Shell
rules.description: Detects msiexec.exe spawning cmd.exe or PowerShell, indicating AlwaysInstallElevated MSI abuse for privilege escalation.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage ends with msiexec.exe; NewProcessName ends with cmd.exe or powershell.exe
rules.false_positives: Legitimate MSI post-install actions

rules.title: Bitsadmin Download File
rules.technique: Signed Binary Proxy Execution: BITS Jobs
rules.description: Bitsadmin used to download files — living-off-the-land download cradle alternative to certutil.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with bitsadmin.exe; CommandLine contains /transfer or /download and http
rules.false_positives: Legitimate BITS-based software updates

rules.title: Capture WiFi Credentials via Netsh
rules.description: Netsh used to export WiFi profiles including cleartext passwords.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with netsh.exe; CommandLine contains wlan and show and profile and key=clear
rules.false_positives: Network administrator WiFi troubleshooting

rules.title: Conhost.exe CommandLine Path Traversal
rules.description: Detects conhost.exe launched with suspicious path traversal in CommandLine — used to launch malicious payloads disguised as console host.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with conhost.exe; CommandLine contains ../ or ..\ patterns outside normal conhost invocation
rules.false_positives: Rare legitimate nested console scenarios

rules.title: CreateDump Process Dump
rules.description: Microsoft's createdump.exe used to dump process memory including LSASS for offline credential extraction.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with createdump.exe; CommandLine contains lsass or target PID
rules.false_positives: Legitimate .NET runtime crash dump generation

rules.title: CSharp-Based BOF Execution via RunDLL32
rules.description: Detects rundll32 executing C# BOF (Beacon Object File) DLLs from memory-only paths or temp directories.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with rundll32.exe; CommandLine contains DotNetToJScript or unusual export name
rules.false_positives: Legitimate rundll32 COM operations

rules.title: DNS Exfiltration and Tunneling Tools Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of known DNS tunneling tools: dnscat2, iodine, dns2tcp, ozymandns.
rules.key_conditions: Image or CommandLine contains dnscat or iodine or dns2tcp or OzymandDNS
rules.false_positives: Authorized DNS research/pentesting

rules.title: Dotnet.EXE Arbitrary DLL or Csproj Code Execution
rules.description: Dotnet.exe used to execute arbitrary .NET code or DLLs without standard PowerShell or csc.exe.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with dotnet.exe; CommandLine contains .dll or .csproj from unusual path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate .NET SDK operations

rules.title: Esentutl Stealth Selective File Copy
rules.description: Esentutl.exe used to copy locked or restricted files including SAM database.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with esentutl.exe; CommandLine contains /y and /vssrec or /d and sensitive file paths
rules.false_positives: Legitimate ESE database maintenance

rules.title: Excel.EXE Spawning Unusual Processes
rules.description: Excel spawning scripting engines, network tools, or unexpected processes — macro execution IOC.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage ends with EXCEL.EXE; NewProcessName in [cmd.exe, powershell.exe, wscript.exe, cscript.exe, mshta.exe, certutil.exe]
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Excel automation macros (rare in modern environments)

rules.title: Finger.EXE Download File
rules.description: finger.exe used as LOLBIN to download files from remote host via finger protocol.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with finger.exe; CommandLine contains http or contains @ with IP pattern
rules.false_positives: Legitimate finger protocol use (extremely rare)

rules.title: Fsi.EXE/FsiAnyCPU.EXE Execution
rules.description: F# Interactive (fsi.exe) used to execute arbitrary F# code — LOLBIN for code execution bypassing script block logging.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with fsi.exe or FsiAnyCPU.exe; CommandLine contains .fsx or script content
rules.false_positives: Legitimate F# development

rules.title: GfxDownloadWrapper.EXE File Download
rules.description: Intel GFX download wrapper used as LOLBIN to download arbitrary files from URLs.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with GfxDownloadWrapper.exe; CommandLine contains http
rules.false_positives: Intel graphics driver present (legitimate use only in that context)

rules.title: HH.EXE Execution
rules.technique: System Binary Proxy Execution: Compiled HTML File
rules.description: Windows Help executable hh.exe executing CHM files from unusual locations or remote paths.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with hh.exe; CommandLine contains http:// or path outside standard Help directories
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Windows help file opening

rules.title: LOLBIN Execution from Unusual Path
rules.description: Any known LOLBIN (regsvr32, mshta, wscript, cscript, rundll32) executing from non-standard path — indicates renamed or relocated binary.
rules.key_conditions: Image OriginalFileName matches LOLBIN but Image path is not in System32 or SysWOW64
rules.false_positives: Rare legitimate scenario

rules.title: Mavinject.EXE Inject into Running Process
rules.description: Mavinject.exe (Microsoft App-V injection utility) used to inject DLLs into running processes.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with mavinject.exe or mavinject32.exe; CommandLine contains /INJECTRUNNING
rules.false_positives: Very rare legitimate App-V use

rules.title: Microsoft HTML Application Spawning Process
rules.description: MSHTA spawning any child process — indicates successful HTA payload execution.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage ends with mshta.exe; any child process created
rules.false_positives: Legitimate HTA-based admin tools

rules.title: Netsh Port Forwarding
rules.description: Netsh used to create port forwarding rules — attackers use this to redirect traffic for tunneling or lateral movement.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with netsh.exe; CommandLine contains portproxy or interface portproxy add
rules.false_positives: Legitimate network administration port forwarding

rules.title: New Firewall Rule Added Via Netsh
rules.technique: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify System Firewall
rules.description: Netsh adding new firewall rules to allow inbound connections — attackers open ports for RAT/backdoor communication.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with netsh.exe; CommandLine contains firewall add rule and action=allow and protocol
rules.false_positives: IT operations adding legitimate firewall rules

rules.title: NirSoft Tools Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of NirSoft credential recovery tools (WebBrowserPassView, CredentialsFileView, NetworkPasswordRecovery, etc.) used for credential theft.
rules.key_conditions: Image or OriginalFileName matches known NirSoft tool names; or CommandLine contains nirsoft specific flags
rules.false_positives: Authorized password recovery operations

rules.title: OneNote Spawning Suspicious Process
rules.description: OneNote (.one file) spawning scripting engines or shells — OneNote-based phishing delivery mechanism heavily abused 2022-2024.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage ends with ONENOTE.EXE; NewProcessName in [cmd.exe, powershell.exe, wscript.exe, cscript.exe, mshta.exe]
rules.false_positives: Legitimate OneNote actions

rules.title: PUA - Potential PowerShell Credential Access
rules.description: PowerShell accessing credential stores (Windows Credential Manager, DPAPI) via System.Security namespace.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with powershell.exe; CommandLine contains CredentialCache or Credential.GetNetworkCredential or System.Security.Credentials or Windows.Security.Credentials
rules.false_positives: Legitimate PS credential management scripts

rules.title: PrintBrm.EXE Remote File Copy
rules.description: PrintBrm.exe used to copy files to/from remote UNC paths — LOLBIN for stealthy file transfer.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with PrintBrm.exe; CommandLine contains /F and \\ (UNC path) and /D
rules.false_positives: Legitimate print queue backup/restore operations

rules.title: Python Spawning Unusual Child Process
rules.description: Python interpreter spawning unexpected child processes — malicious Python scripts, reverse shells, or tool execution.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage ends with python.exe or python3.exe; NewProcessName in [cmd.exe, powershell.exe] or outbound network tools
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Python scripts spawning subprocesses

rules.title: Reg.EXE Flags Abuse
rules.description: Reg.exe used with flags to save/restore/load/unload registry hives — used to exfiltrate SAM, SYSTEM, SECURITY hives offline.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with reg.exe; CommandLine contains save or export and sam or system or security; or CommandLine contains load
rules.false_positives: Legitimate registry backup/restore operations

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - GoToAssist Execution
rules.description: GoToAssist remote access tool execution — legitimate tool abused for unauthorized remote access.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with G2AService.exe or GoToAssist.exe or g2ax_comm_customer.exe
rules.false_positives: Authorized IT support use of GoToAssist

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - NetSupportManager Execution
rules.description: NetSupport Manager execution — heavily abused by TA577, Conti, IcedID campaigns as post-exploitation RAT.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with client32.exe (NetSupport); or CommandLine contains Gateway and NetSupport
rules.false_positives: Authorized IT use of NetSupport

rules.title: Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Execution
rules.description: ScreenConnect (ConnectWise Control) execution — used in multiple ransomware campaigns (LockBit, BlackCat) for persistent remote access. CVE-2024-1709 exploitation indicator when spawning unusual children.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe or ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe spawning unusual child processes
rules.false_positives: Authorized ScreenConnect deployments

rules.title: SafeBoot Key Registry Modification
rules.description: Reg.exe adding keys to SafeBoot registry — ransomware technique to persist in safe mode where security tools don't load.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with reg.exe; CommandLine contains add and HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SafeBoot
rules.false_positives: Very rare legitimate safe mode configuration

rules.title: Scheduled Task Created Via SchtasksNT
rules.description: Schtasks.exe creating tasks with suspicious AT-style naming or system-impersonating task names.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with schtasks.exe; CommandLine contains /create and (/ru SYSTEM or /rl HIGHEST) and unusual TaskName patterns
rules.false_positives: Legitimate system task creation

rules.title: Script Interpreter Execution From Suspicious Folder
rules.description: Scripting interpreters (wscript.exe, cscript.exe) executing scripts from temp, downloads, or unusual paths.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with wscript.exe or cscript.exe; CommandLine contains script path in Temp, Downloads, AppData, or ProgramData
rules.false_positives: Legitimate scripts in unusual locations (software installers)

rules.title: Squiblydoo - Regsvr32 Loading Remote Scriptlet
rules.description: Regsvr32 loading a COM scriptlet from a remote URL or UNC path (Squiblydoo AppLocker bypass).
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with regsvr32.exe; CommandLine contains /s /u /i: and http:// or \\
rules.false_positives: Legitimate regsvr32 COM registration (rare with remote paths)

rules.title: Suspicious Advpack DLL
rules.description: Rundll32 executing advpack.dll or setupapi.dll functions used to run INF install scripts for arbitrary code execution.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with rundll32.exe; CommandLine contains advpack.dll,LaunchINFSection or setupapi.dll,InstallHinfSection
rules.false_positives: Legitimate software installation using INF files

rules.title: Suspicious Cmstp Execution
rules.description: Cmstp.exe (Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer) executing .inf file from network share — UAC bypass and AppLocker bypass technique.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with cmstp.exe; CommandLine contains /s and (/ns or /au or .inf file from network path)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate VPN connection profile installation

rules.title: Suspicious Desktopimgdownldr.EXE Download
rules.description: Desktopimgdownldr.exe (lock screen image downloader) abused to download arbitrary files from the internet.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with desktopimgdownldr.exe; CommandLine contains /lockscreenurl: and http
rules.false_positives: No legitimate use with arbitrary URLs

rules.title: Suspicious Diantz Alternate Data Stream
rules.description: Diantz.exe used to compress files into alternate data streams (ADS) to hide malicious content.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with diantz.exe; CommandLine contains :\\<filename>:<stream> (ADS notation)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate cabinet file creation

rules.title: Suspicious DiskShadow Usage
rules.description: DiskShadow.exe used with script file — can proxy execution of arbitrary commands via VSS shadow copy operations.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with diskshadow.exe; CommandLine contains /s (script mode) and unusual script path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate VSS management operations

rules.title: Suspicious Double Extension File Execution
rules.description: Execution of files with double extensions (e.g., invoice.pdf.exe, document.docx.scr) used to trick users.
rules.key_conditions: Image or CommandLine contains filename pattern \.(pdf|doc|xls|jpg|png)\.(exe|scr|bat|com|vbs|js)
rules.false_positives: None — double extension is nearly always malicious

rules.title: Suspicious Findstr.EXE Usage
rules.description: Findstr.exe used to search for sensitive strings (passwords, credentials, private keys) or to download remote files.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with findstr.exe; CommandLine contains /s /i and (password or secret or key); or CommandLine contains http:// (download via findstr)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate code/log searching

rules.title: Suspicious Ftp.EXE Execution
rules.description: FTP client (ftp.exe) used for file transfer or as script execution proxy — rare in modern environments.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with ftp.exe; CommandLine contains -s: (script) or -n (no auto-login) with unusual server
rules.false_positives: Legitimate FTP transfers (rare in enterprise)

rules.title: Suspicious GrpConv.EXE Execution
rules.description: GrpConv.exe used to execute malicious .grp files containing arbitrary commands.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with grpconv.exe; CommandLine contains .grp file from unusual path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Program Manager group conversion (Windows 3.x era — very rare)

rules.title: Suspicious Homedrive Env Variable Usage
rules.description: Malware using HOMEDRIVE environment variable to construct paths that evade static path-based detection.
rules.key_conditions: CommandLine contains %HOMEDRIVE% with script/executable reference outside normal user profile locations
rules.false_positives: Some legitimate user scripts use HOMEDRIVE

rules.title: Suspicious Ieexec.EXE Usage
rules.description: IEExec.exe (IE execution helper) used to download and execute arbitrary .NET applications from URLs.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with ieexec.exe; CommandLine contains http:// or https://
rules.false_positives: No legitimate modern use

rules.title: Suspicious Infdefaultinstall.EXE Usage
rules.description: Infdefaultinstall.exe executing INF scripts to run arbitrary commands — AppLocker and UAC bypass.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with InfDefaultInstall.exe; CommandLine contains .inf file from unusual path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate INF file installation

rules.title: Suspicious Invoke-WebRequest Usage
rules.description: PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest or wget/curl aliases used to download malicious payloads.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with powershell.exe; CommandLine contains Invoke-WebRequest or iwr or wget or curl and -OutFile or -Uri and http
rules.false_positives: Legitimate PS-based software download

rules.title: Suspicious Msiexec Quiet Install
rules.description: Msiexec executing MSI from remote URL or unusual path with /quiet flag — silent malware installation.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with msiexec.exe; CommandLine contains /i and http:// and (/quiet or /q)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate silent software deployment via MSI

rules.title: Suspicious MSODiagnostics Execution
rules.description: Microsoft Office diagnostic tool abused to proxy DLL execution or download content.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with MSODiagnostics.exe; CommandLine with unusual arguments or URL references
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Office diagnostics

rules.title: Suspicious Parent of Csc.EXE
rules.description: Csc.exe (C# compiler) invoked by suspicious parents — in-memory compilation of malicious C# payloads.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage in [wscript.exe, mshta.exe, outlook.exe, winword.exe]; NewProcessName ends with csc.exe
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Office add-in compilation (rare)

rules.title: Suspicious PowerShell Download Activity
rules.description: PowerShell downloading content from file sharing domains (Discord CDN, file.io, transfer.sh, paste sites) commonly used for payload hosting.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with powershell.exe; CommandLine contains (discord.com/attachments or cdn.discordapp.com or transfer.sh or file.io or hastebin.com) and (Invoke-WebRequest or DownloadFile or DownloadString)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Discord/file sharing in enterprise (rare)

rules.title: Suspicious Regasm/Regsvcs Execution
rules.description: Regasm.exe and Regsvcs.exe used to execute arbitrary .NET assembly code via COM registration — AppLocker bypass.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with regasm.exe or regsvcs.exe; CommandLine contains .dll or .exe from unusual path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate .NET assembly COM registration

rules.title: Suspicious Rundll32 No Arguments
rules.description: Rundll32 spawned without arguments — Cobalt Strike and similar frameworks use argumentless rundll32 as sacrificial process for injection.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with rundll32.exe; CommandLine is empty or contains only rundll32.exe with no additional arguments
rules.false_positives: Very rare legitimate use

rules.title: Suspicious SC.EXE Usage
rules.technique: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
rules.description: Sc.exe creating or modifying services with unusual binary paths — service hijacking or new malicious service installation.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with sc.exe; CommandLine contains create and binpath= with unusual path (temp, appdata)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate service management

rules.title: Suspicious Scrobj.EXE Execution
rules.description: Scrobj.dll (Windows Script Component runtime) used to execute WSC script files — AppLocker bypass technique.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with scrobj.exe or CommandLine contains scrobj.dll and scriptlet
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Windows script component use

rules.title: Suspicious Scriptrunner Execution
rules.description: Scriptrunner.exe used as LOLBIN to execute arbitrary commands masquerading as a legitimate script runner.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with Scriptrunner.exe; CommandLine contains -appvscript or unusual script path
rules.false_positives: No documented legitimate use as general script runner

rules.title: Suspicious ShellExec_RunDLL Usage
rules.description: Rundll32 executing shell32.dll ShellExec_RunDLL to launch URLs or files while bypassing standard execution monitoring.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with rundll32.exe; CommandLine contains shell32.dll,ShellExec_RunDLL and http:// or unusual file path
rules.false_positives: Rare legitimate shell launching

rules.title: Suspicious SyncAppvPublishingServer Execution
rules.description: SyncAppvPublishingServer.exe or .vbs used to execute PowerShell commands bypassing execution policy.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with SyncAppvPublishingServer.exe or SyncAppvPublishingServer.vbs; CommandLine contains PowerShell commands
rules.false_positives: Legitimate App-V publishing

rules.title: Suspicious Unused RunDLL32 Shell32 Function
rules.description: Rundll32 calling obscure undocumented Shell32 functions used for process spoofing and execution.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with rundll32.exe; CommandLine contains shell32.dll and (Control_RunDLL or OpenAs_RunDLL or printui.dll)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate control panel applet launching

rules.title: Suspicious Use of Net.EXE — Domain Recon
rules.description: Net.exe commands for AD reconnaissance — net user /domain, net group /domain, net accounts /domain.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with net.exe or net1.exe; CommandLine contains /domain and (user or group or accounts)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate admin account queries

rules.title: Suspicious XwizardDLL Execution
rules.description: Xwizard.exe used to load arbitrary DLLs — LOLBIN technique abusing wizard framework.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with xwizard.exe; CommandLine contains unusual CLSID or DLL path not in standard Windows locations
rules.false_positives: Very rare legitimate wizard invocation

rules.title: Sysmon Driver Unload via fltmc.exe
rules.description: Fltmc.exe used to unload the Sysmon minifilter driver, blinding endpoint monitoring.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with fltmc.exe; CommandLine contains unload and SysmonDrv
rules.false_positives: No legitimate reason to unload Sysmon driver

rules.title: Trickbot Malware Recon Activity
rules.description: Pattern of recon commands characteristic of TrickBot/BazarLoader post-exploitation activity.
rules.key_conditions: CommandLine sequence: ipconfig/all, net view /all, net view /all /domain, nltest /domain_trusts executed in rapid succession from same parent
rules.false_positives: Legitimate admin recon (very similar patterns, tune by context)

rules.title: Unusual Parent Process for cmd.exe
rules.technique: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell
rules.description: Cmd.exe launched from unusual parent processes that don't normally spawn cmd — browser, mail client, or system process.
rules.key_conditions: NewProcessName ends with cmd.exe; ParentImage ends with [chrome.exe, firefox.exe, OUTLOOK.EXE, iexplore.exe, explorer.exe] with suspicious CommandLine arguments
rules.false_positives: Legitimate cmd invocation from some applications

rules.title: VsCode Tunnel Persistence
rules.description: Visual Studio Code tunnel used for persistent remote access — 'code tunnel' creates an authenticated reverse tunnel attackers abuse.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with code.exe or code-tunnel.exe; CommandLine contains tunnel
rules.false_positives: Legitimate developer VS Code remote development

rules.title: Wmic Uninstall Security Software
rules.technique: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
rules.description: WMIC used to uninstall security software including AV, EDR agents.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with wmic.exe; CommandLine contains product and where and call uninstall and (antivirus or endpoint or defender or security)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate software removal via WMIC

rules.title: XCopy Executable Lateral Movement
rules.description: XCopy used to copy executables to remote admin shares — lateral movement file staging technique.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with xcopy.exe; CommandLine contains \\ (UNC path) and .exe; DestinationPath includes ADMIN$ or C$
rules.false_positives: Legitimate xcopy-based software deployment

rules.title: Remote Desktop from Non-Standard Tool
rules.description: Non-RDP-client processes initiating RDP connections (port 3389) — indicates RDP tunneling or custom RDP tool.
rules.key_conditions: Initiated=true; DestinationPort=3389; Image not in [mstsc.exe, msrdc.exe, known RDP clients]
rules.false_positives: Security scanners, RDP management tools

rules.title: WannaCry Ransomware Network Propagation
rules.description: Detects network scanning on port 445 (SMB) characteristic of WannaCry/NotPetya and similar worm propagation.
rules.key_conditions: Initiated=true; DestinationPort=445; Image not in known system processes; high connection rate to diverse destination IPs
rules.false_positives: Network security scanners, legitimate SMB traffic

rules.title: Adfind Network Connection
rules.description: AdFind making LDAP connections to domain controllers (port 389/636/3268) — confirms active AD enumeration.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with adfind.exe or AdFind.exe; Initiated=true; DestinationPort in [389,636,3268,3269]
rules.false_positives: No legitimate non-AD-recon use of AdFind against DC

rules.title: SharpHound Network Connection to DC
rules.description: SharpHound (BloodHound collector) making LDAP/SMB connections to domain controllers.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with SharpHound.exe or matches compiled collector binary; DestinationPort in [389,445,636]
rules.false_positives: Authorized BloodHound runs during assessment

rules.title: Potential C2 Reverse Shell Connection
rules.description: Outbound TCP connection from PowerShell or cmd.exe with very small payload size and regular intervals — reverse shell beacon pattern.
rules.key_conditions: Image in [powershell.exe, cmd.exe]; Initiated=true; DestinationPort not in [80,443,53,8080]; DestinationIp is external
rules.false_positives: Development and testing activities

rules.title: FTP Connection from Unusual Process
rules.description: Non-FTP-client process making FTP connections (port 21) — data exfiltration via FTP from unexpected tools.
rules.key_conditions: Initiated=true; DestinationPort=21; Image not in [ftp.exe, winscp.exe, filezilla.exe, known FTP clients]
rules.false_positives: Enterprise FTP servers receiving legitimate connections

rules.title: Assembly Loaded from Suspicious Path
rules.description: Unsigned .NET assembly loaded from temp or user-writable directory — reflective loading or side-load attack.
rules.key_conditions: ImageLoaded path in Temp or AppData or ProgramData; Signed=false; extension is .dll or .clr
rules.false_positives: Legitimate software with DLLs in AppData (e.g., Chrome extensions)

rules.title: Known Credential Access DLL Loaded
rules.description: Known credential access tool DLL (samlib.dll, vaultcli.dll, wdigest.dll) loaded into unusual process.
rules.key_conditions: ImageLoaded ends with samlib.dll or vaultcli.dll or wdigest.dll; Image not in expected system processes
rules.false_positives: Normal Windows credential operations

rules.title: Remote Access Tool DLL Load
rules.description: Known RMM tool DLL loaded — indicates RMM tool installation or execution even if the main binary is renamed.
rules.key_conditions: ImageLoaded ends with known RMM DLL names (anydesk.dll, TeamViewer.dll, ScreenConnect DLLs)
rules.false_positives: Authorized RMM tool deployments

rules.title: Credential Dumping via Mimikatz GrantedAccess Mask
rules.description: High-precision Mimikatz detection via specific GrantedAccess masks and CallTrace indicating sekurlsa module.
rules.key_conditions: TargetImage ends with lsass.exe; GrantedAccess=0x1010 or 0x1410; CallTrace contains unknown module (shellcode)
rules.false_positives: AV/EDR monitoring of LSASS

rules.title: SAM Database Handle Request
rules.description: Process opening handle to SAM database hive — credential dumping targeting local account hashes.
rules.key_conditions: TargetImage ends with lsass.exe; GrantedAccess contains 0x40 or 0x1000; CallTrace contains samlib.dll
rules.false_positives: Backup software, security auditing tools

rules.title: Malicious Document Macro Drop
rules.description: Office application writing script files (.js, .vbs, .hta, .bat) to disk — macro dropper activity.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with WINWORD.EXE or EXCEL.EXE; TargetFilename ends with .js or .vbs or .bat or .hta or .ps1
rules.false_positives: Legitimate macro-based reporting tools (rare)

rules.title: Webshell Drop by Web Server
rules.description: Web server process (IIS, Apache, Tomcat) creating script files in web directories — webshell upload.
rules.key_conditions: ParentImage in [w3wp.exe, httpd.exe, tomcat.exe, nginx.exe]; TargetFilename ends with .aspx or .php or .jsp and web directory path
rules.false_positives: Legitimate web application deployments

rules.title: Ransomware Ransom Note File Created
rules.description: Creation of files matching common ransomware ransom note naming patterns (HOW_TO_DECRYPT, README_DECRYPT, RESTORE_FILES).
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename matches patterns: *HOW_TO* or *DECRYPT* or *RESTORE* or *README*_*ransom* or *YOUR_FILES*; created in bulk across multiple directories
rules.false_positives: None — these file names are highly specific to ransomware

rules.title: Suspicious Executable File Extension Masquerade
rules.description: File created with executable content but non-executable extension — masquerading technique to evade extension-based detection.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .txt or .log or .dat or .tmp; Hashes indicates PE header; created in temp directories
rules.false_positives: Log files and temp data that happen to start with MZ (rare)

rules.title: AMSI Provider DLL Drop
rules.description: New DLL dropped to path associated with AMSI (Antimalware Scan Interface) providers — AMSI bypass via rogue provider registration.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename path contains AmsiProvider or AMSI; extension is .dll; Image not in Windows installer processes
rules.false_positives: Legitimate security product AMSI provider updates

rules.title: COM Object Hijacking via Registry
rules.description: HKCU CLSID registration overriding HKLM CLSID — COM object hijacking for persistence without admin privileges.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains HKCU\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID; EventType=CreateKey or SetValue; new InProcServer32 or LocalServer32 pointing to unusual DLL
rules.false_positives: Legitimate user-space COM registration (rare)

rules.title: Credential Manager Registry Modification
rules.description: Registry modification to Credential Manager settings — disabling credential guard or modifying vault behavior.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains \Control\Lsa\DisableRestrictedAdmin or \CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders; EventType=SetValue; unusual value
rules.false_positives: Security policy configuration by admins

rules.title: Disable Security Center Notifications
rules.description: Registry modification disabling Windows Security Center notifications — attackers disable to prevent user awareness of disabled security controls.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Security Center\Notifications\DisableNotify; Details=1
rules.false_positives: Group Policy-based notification management

rules.title: NetNTLM Downgrade Attack
rules.description: Registry modification to LMCompatibilityLevel — forcing weaker NTLM authentication for credential capture.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains \CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\LmCompatibilityLevel; Details set to 0,1,2 (reduced from 3+)
rules.false_positives: Legacy system compatibility requirements

rules.title: PortProxy Registry Entry
rules.description: Registry entry for Windows port proxy (netsh portproxy) — persistent port forwarding for C2 or lateral movement.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains \CurrentControlSet\Services\PortProxy\v4tov4
rules.false_positives: Legitimate network routing configuration

rules.title: Remote Desktop Shadowing Registry
rules.description: Registry modification enabling Remote Desktop Shadowing — allows silently viewing/interacting with user sessions without consent.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains Terminal Server\AllowMultiTSInstances or Shadow; value enabling shadowing without consent
rules.false_positives: IT support remote assistance

rules.title: Sysmon Configuration Tampering via Registry
rules.description: Direct registry modification of Sysmon configuration parameters — bypassing normal sysmon.exe config update to tamper with rules.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\SysmonDrv; EventType=SetValue; Image not sysmon.exe or System
rules.false_positives: No legitimate use case outside Sysmon itself

rules.title: Sticky Keys Backdoor Registry
rules.description: IFEO debugger set for accessibility binaries (sethc.exe, utilman.exe, osk.exe) — creates backdoor shell at login screen.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains Image File Execution Options\sethc.exe or utilman.exe or osk.exe or Magnify.exe; Debugger value set
rules.false_positives: Security testing in lab environments

rules.title: Windows Subsystem Linux Registry Modification
rules.description: WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) registry modification enabling or configuring WSL — attackers use Linux side to evade Windows detection.
rules.key_conditions: TargetObject contains HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Lxss or \kernel\lxss; unexpected enabling
rules.false_positives: Legitimate developer enabling WSL

rules.title: Macro-Enabled Office Document Downloaded
rules.description: Office documents with macro capability (.xlsm, .xlam, .docm, .dotm, .pptm) downloaded from internet.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .xlsm or .xlam or .docm or .dotm or .pptm or .xlsb; Zone.Identifier stream with ZoneId=3
rules.false_positives: Legitimate macro-enabled business documents from trusted sources

rules.title: Potentially Malicious File Extension Downloaded
