rules.description: High-risk file types downloaded from internet: CHM, HTA, MSI, VHDX, WSF, WSH.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .chm or .hta or .msi or .vhdx or .wsf or .wsh; Zone.Identifier with ZoneId=3
rules.false_positives: Legitimate software distributed via these formats

rules.title: Metasploit Meterpreter Named Pipe
rules.description: Meterpreter reverse shell named pipe patterns — default and common custom pipe names.
rules.key_conditions: PipeName matches \\pipe\\meterpreter* or \\pipe\\msf* or \\pipe\\met*; or random 8+ character pipe names created by unusual processes
rules.false_positives: Security testing tools in authorized red team ops

rules.title: Impacket Tool Named Pipe Activity
rules.description: Impacket framework named pipe communication patterns — smbexec and psexec.py create characteristic service names and pipes.
rules.key_conditions: PipeName matches \\pipe\\ATSVC or patterns from Impacket SMBExec default service naming; Image=services.exe creating pipe with random-looking name
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Windows service communication

rules.title: DNS Query for Tor Onion Address
rules.description: DNS query for .onion domain indicating Tor usage — C2 over Tor or dark web resource access.
rules.key_conditions: QueryName ends with .onion; any process
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Tor Browser use (rare in enterprise)

rules.title: Crypto Miner DNS Query
rules.description: DNS queries to known cryptocurrency mining pool domains — cryptojacking activity.
rules.key_conditions: QueryName ends with known mining pool domains (xmrpool.eu, pool.minexmr.com, miningpoolhub.com, nanopool.org, etc.)
rules.false_positives: Legitimate cryptocurrency trading or monitoring software

rules.title: Dynamic DNS Resolution
rules.description: DNS queries to dynamic DNS providers (duckdns.org, no-ip.com, ddns.net, hopto.org) — C2 infrastructure using free dynamic DNS to avoid blocklisting.
rules.key_conditions: QueryName ends with duckdns.org or no-ip.com or ddns.net or hopto.org or redirectme.net or serveftp.com
rules.false_positives: Legitimate home-lab dynamic DNS use

rules.title: Microsoft Office Product DNS Anomaly
rules.description: Unusual DNS query from Office applications to non-Microsoft domains — potential document-embedded C2 beacon.
rules.key_conditions: Image in [WINWORD.EXE, EXCEL.EXE, POWERPNT.EXE]; QueryName not ending with microsoft.com or office.com or live.com or windows.com
rules.false_positives: Legitimate Office template and content checks

rules.title: Known LOLDriver Hash Loaded
rules.description: Driver with known-vulnerable hash from loldrivers.io blocklist — BYOVD attack using legitimate signed-but-vulnerable driver.
rules.key_conditions: ImageLoaded hash (SHA256/MD5) matches loldrivers.io blocklist; Signed=true but known vulnerable
rules.false_positives: Very rare — legitimate old driver on legacy system

rules.title: Windows Kernel Vulnerable Driver Load
rules.description: Known kernel vulnerability exploitation driver loaded — commonly seen in EDR killer tools (AuKill, Terminator, etc.).
rules.key_conditions: ImageLoaded matches known EDR-killer driver filenames or hashes (ProcExp.sys from expired cert, truesight.sys, daxin.sys)
rules.false_positives: No legitimate use of these specific drivers

rules.title: WMI Persistence via MOF File
rules.description: WMI event subscription created via MOF (Managed Object Format) file compilation — less common but effective persistence method.
rules.key_conditions: EventType=CreateInstance; Operation contains __EventFilter or __EventConsumer; Consumer type CommandLineEventConsumer with MofFilePath
rules.false_positives: Legitimate WMI management through MOF files (enterprise management software)

rules.title: SilentCleanup Scheduled Task Abuse for Persistence
rules.description: WMI event consumer triggering SilentCleanup task for UAC bypass or persistence — well-known auto-elevating task abuse.
rules.key_conditions: Consumer Destination contains schtasks and SilentCleanup; or Filter query targets SilentCleanup execution window
rules.false_positives: No legitimate use of WMI to trigger SilentCleanup

rules.title: Herpaderping Process Tampering
rules.description: Process herpaderping — writing malicious content to file after section is mapped, before security tools scan it. EID 25 Type='Image is locked for access'.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=25; Type='Image is locked for access'
rules.false_positives: None — this is a clear evasion technique

rules.title: Process Hollowing via Svchost
rules.description: Process hollowing targeting svchost.exe — malicious code running inside the service host while appearing legitimate. EID 25 Type='Image is replaced'.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=25; Type='Image is replaced'; Image ends with svchost.exe or any trusted system binary
rules.false_positives: Very rare legitimate scenario

rules.title: Potential Ransomware Shadow Copy Raw Access
rules.description: Raw access to shadow copy volume — ransomware reading VSS to find and delete backup copies.
rules.key_conditions: Device contains HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy; Image not in backup software allowlist
rules.false_positives: Legitimate backup software accessing shadow copies

rules.title: Timestomp via Powershell Set-Date
rules.description: PowerShell Set-Date or .NET [System.IO.File]::SetCreationTime used to modify file timestamps — scripted timestomping.
rules.key_conditions: Image ends with powershell.exe; CommandLine contains Set-Date or SetCreationTime or SetLastWriteTime or SetLastAccessTime
rules.false_positives: Legitimate file management scripts

rules.title: Backup Catalog Deletion
rules.description: Deletion of Windows backup catalog files — attackers delete to prevent recovery from Windows Backup.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .wbcat or contains WindowsImageBackup or BCD or boot
rules.false_positives: No legitimate reason to delete backup catalog outside administrative tasks

rules.title: Log File Deletion
rules.technique: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs
rules.description: Deletion of Windows event log files (.evtx) or log directories — evidence destruction.
rules.key_conditions: TargetFilename ends with .evtx; or TargetFilename path contains \Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\
rules.false_positives: No legitimate reason to directly delete .evtx files (use wevtutil cl instead)

rules.title: Executable File Created by Script Interpreter
rules.description: PE executable created by scripting engine (wscript, cscript, mshta) — dropper script delivering payload.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=29; Image ends with wscript.exe or cscript.exe or mshta.exe or powershell.exe; TargetFilename ends with .exe or .dll
rules.false_positives: Legitimate installers written as scripts that extract executables

rules.title: Executable Written to Suspicious Path by Process
rules.description: PE written directly to an autostart location — direct persistence without registry modification.
rules.key_conditions: EventID=29; TargetFilename path in [\Startup\, \Start Menu\, C:\Windows\, C:\Windows\System32\]; Image not in [known installers, TrustedInstaller.exe, System]
rules.false_positives: Legitimate software installation to system directories
